statement.focus_question: Can one classify all solutions of\[\prod_{1\leq i\leq k_1}(m_1+i)=\prod_{1\leq j\leq k_2}(m_2+j)\]where $k_1,k_2>3$ and $m_1+k_1\leq m_2$?
statement.normalized: Can one classify all solutions of\[\prod_{1\leq i\leq k_1}(m_1+i)=\prod_{1\leq j\leq k_2}(m_2+j)\]where $k_1,k_2>3$ and $m_1+k_1\leq m_2$? Are there only finitely many solutions?
statement.original: Can one classify all solutions of\[\prod_{1\leq i\leq k_1}(m_1+i)=\prod_{1\leq j\leq k_2}(m_2+j)\]where $k_1,k_2>3$ and $m_1+k_1\leq m_2$? Are there only finitely many solutions?
